title: Database Access on SQLite
text: Database Access on SQLite The SLE connector uses the embedded SQLite library to access and manipulate the DBMS file. Database Path Specify the logon path for the connector as follows: $SLE SLE: database Copy where database is a file path assignment, possibly containing Uniface path logicals. For example: $SLE SLE: C:/Uniface_Data/project/dbms/usys.db Copy The SLE connector does not check for a user name or password. Using SQLite for the Repository Important: SQLite can be used as Repository in a stand-alone environment. It should not be used as a shared Repository. When used as a Repository, the Uniface system information assignments $IDF, $UUU, $SYS and $DEF can all be assigned to the SLE connector. For example, the following is a typical $IDF is the assignment for the Repository. If SQLite is used, it is advisable to create at least a four MB application database to hold the application definition and the data. Example: Basic Configuration SQLite is the installed as the default database on Windows, using the following configuration in the assignment file. [DRIVER_SETTINGS] SLE U2.0 USYS$SLE_PARAMS create db = on [PATHS] ; Path to the SQLite database for the Uniface Repository: $DBMS SLE: UnifaceDataFolder /project/dbms/usys.db ; Path to the SQLite database for User defined data: $DBMS_DEF SLE: UnifaceDataFolder /project/dbms/userdata.db $SYS $DBMS $UUU $DBMS $IDF $DBMS $DEF $DBMS_DEF Copy Related Links create db If the database file does not yet exist, creates a database file in the path specified by the connector setting.
